name: persona-interview-scope-routing
ground_truth_note: Scripted-persona harness: the headless session runs discover AND role-plays 'Sam', a plain-register non-technical gym owner, answering discover's questions in character. This tests the STRUCTURAL interview logic that survives self-play (tier behavior, routing, teach-back, FRD completeness), NOT the adaptive logic (register mirroring / genuine gap discovery are contaminated because the model wrote the persona). Planted behaviors to grade from the FRD (discover records its interview into the artifact by design): (1) SCOPE-CREEP GUARDRAIL — Sam's 'exercise search is slow' aside must land in ## Suggested Follow-ups, never in Goals/Requirements (the strongest single interview-logic test); (2) CONSTRAINT CAPTURE — 'must not block the plan / don't change generation' lands in Constraints; (3) CONSENSUS — register + consensus frontmatter present (classification + teach-back ran), content_hash stamped (finalization lint); (4) FRD COMPLETENESS — all template sections present. Discover uses status: complete (not ready), so validate_artifact:true is NOT usable (same as research). Run in wt-head3 so discover's Step-3 codebase probe hits real GymApp code.

section_routing.note: Sam's slow-exercise-search aside routes to Follow-ups, never into the feature's positive scope (Goals/Requirements/Acceptance). Non-Goals is NOT forbidden — an explicit out-of-scope exclusion there is correct.
the 'must not block the plan / don't change generation' constraint is captured in Constraints
